headline: three patterns, one cost dial
sub: Relative token cost, not a benchmark. The one real number: the answer pattern runs ~60% lighter per lookup on a doc-heavy repo.

cols.head: NOT
cols.body: not code search. not for a 5-file repo. won't make a bad model good.

cols.head: IS
cols.body: one current answer instead of six stale docs, for doc-heavy repos that drift.

headline: does a context-router replace your vector db? mostly no
sub: It doesn't replace rag or grep. It's one canonical answer for a doc-heavy repo that drifts.

headline: moved the savings receipt to where you actually look
sub: From a buried log to the end of every run. If the number is hidden, it may as well not exist.

headline: copilot is 10%, operator is 90%

data.label: operator (read repo, open PR, call tools)

sub: Our estimate, not a benchmark. The work that moves the needle is the operator surface, not the autocomplete.

headline: the speed you feel from AI today is a loan
sub: GitClear, 211M lines: copy-paste rose 8.3% to 12.3%, refactoring fell 24.1% to 9.5%. The interest comes due later.
